name: Online Meetup
description: We’re meeting on Discord! The link to join the Baltimore Indie Game Devs discord server will be in your RSVP message. Join us to discuss game development, talk shop, and bounce ideas. Anyone with an interest in game design and development is welcome, from beginners to veteran professionals. If you’re working on a game, you’re […]

name: Dev Mode
description: DevMode is a new gathering for Baltimore-based game developers, digital artists, creative technologists, and tech-minded entrepreneurs to connect, network, and spark ideas. Whether you’re building AAA titles, experimenting in Unreal or Unity, or exploring how your skills might translate into the gaming space, this is for YOU. Meet local devs, test-play featured games, and mingle with […]

name: In-Person/Online Meetup (Version Control Certification)
description: In-person is at the East Columbia Branch Library in the Marvin Thomas Room. Join us IN-PERSON or ONLINE. Anyone with an interest in game design and development is welcome, from beginners to veteran professionals. Julian Coy will run a Version Control Basic Proficiency certification session from 10:30 AM – 11:30 AM. Learn how to use version control to work with teams. […]

name: Game Escape Indie Showcase
description: Come see a selection of locally made indie games brought to you by the creators behind them! A curation of locally made games presented by the developers behind them! Featuring a performance by the BGSO Small Ensembles at 1-2 PM! Parking is available in limited quantities on the street, and the recommended parking lot is […]

name: Ludum Dare 58
description: Ludum Dare is an online event where games are made from scratch in a weekend. BIG is planning to host an in-person site for people who want to get together in person to make games. Location details will be announced in early September. ldjam.com | Ludum Dare game jam

name: Game Your Future Conference
description: This is the 4th annual Game Your Future is a conference, an event organized by Maryland-regional developer groups at the Baltimore Convention Center.  It brings an experience similar to the Game Developers Conference (GDC) to the East Coast to fill the void of a more accessible event for our East Coast devs who want something […]
